steps.title: Identify the tense in the 'Because' clause
steps.content: The subordinate clause begins with “Because both languages require...” using present tense. The blank completes a cause-and-effect statement about general conditions, not a specific past event.

steps.title: Choose present tense to match
steps.content: Present-tense struggle maintains the cause-and-effect relationship established by the “Because” clause. The three past forms (struggled, were struggling, had struggled) incorrectly shift to past time, contradicting the established frame. That matches choice A — A is correct.
